description_for_human: Scan your Laravel application for security vulnerabilities, analyze controller authorization flows, and apply known CVE patches — all from within GitHub Copilot Chat.
description_for_model: Security analysis agent for Laravel applications. Exposes four callable skills: vulnerabilityScan (static analysis across 13 security categories), analyzeAuthFlow (reflection-based authorization gap detection), applySecurityPatch (CVE-driven file patching), and sanitizeGitHistory (full git history audit and scrubbing via git filter-repo). All calls require a valid Sanctum Bearer token with the 'agent:invoke' ability and the X-Agent-Context session header.

tools.name: vulnerabilityScan
tools.description: Scans a file path for Laravel security vulnerabilities across 13 categories: IDOR, SQL Injection, Mass Assignment, XSS, CSRF, File Uploads, Authorization, Rate Limiting, Session, Security Headers, Credentials, Git Secrets, and Input Validation. Returns findings sorted by severity (critical → important → suggestion).

path.description: Relative path within the Laravel project to scan. Examples: 'app/Http/Controllers', 'app/Models', 'app/'.

tools.name: analyzeAuthFlow
tools.description: Inspects a Laravel controller's public methods using PHP Reflection to detect missing $this->authorize() or Gate::authorize() calls. Returns a per-method authorization gap report, highlighting destructive methods (store, update, destroy) that lack authorization.

controller.description: Controller class name or relative file path. Examples: 'UserController', 'Api/OrderController', 'app/Http/Controllers/UserController.php'.

tools.name: applySecurityPatch
tools.description: Applies a known security patch for a CVE to the specified file. Looks up the CVE in the patch registry (config/security-agent.php), creates a timestamped backup, applies the transformation, and runs post-patch Artisan commands (optimize:clear by default). Returns a diff summary and command results.

cveId.description: CVE identifier for the patch to apply. Must match an entry in config/security-agent.php > patch_registry. Example: 'CVE-2024-1234'.

filePath.description: Relative path to the file that needs patching. Example: 'app/Http/Middleware/VerifyCsrfToken.php'.

tools.name: sanitizeGitHistory
tools.description: Performs a two-phase git history sanitization. Phase 1 (audit) discovers secrets across all branches: APP_KEY (base64:...), DB/Redis/Mail credentials, and internal staging IPs (excluding loopback/private ranges and semver). Phase 2 (scrub) generates and optionally executes a git filter-repo script that purges sensitive files (e.g. .env, deploy.php, *.pem) from full history and blob-replaces secrets with [REDACTED], then runs aggressive GC. Always verifies a backup branch before rewriting.

backupBranch.description: Name of the git branch that must exist before history is rewritten. Defaults to 'backup/before-sanitize'.

dryRun.description: If true (default), only audit and generate the script — do not execute the rewrite.

repoPath.description: Relative path from Laravel root to the git repository. Defaults to the project root.

generateScriptPath.description: Where to write the sanitizer script. Defaults to 'storage/app/sanitize-git-history.sh'.
